_doc: Spec-implementation parity ledger (Section F). One entry per companion/native-channel/mapping specification. It records, honestly, whether each normative document has (a) a reference implementation demonstrated in isolation and (b) machine-gradable non-circular conformance vectors -- OR is a TRACKED Class-B gap with a named owner and a gate. status vocabulary: impl_status = wired | planned ; conformance_status = graded | spec-audited | planned-vectors. A 'spec-audited' or 'planned-vectors' entry is an OPEN, OWNED gap, not a terminal state (NEP-0000 §6: never Final without impl + non-circular vectors). The spec-parity-gate write hook and esb-gate.ps1 read this file; the spec-parity-completion-gate blocks a Final-claiming spec-only commit. Anchors are {file, must_contain}; a 'wired' impl or 'graded' conformance entry whose anchors do not resolve is a broken claim (esb-wiring-gate semantics).

capabilities.name: WIRE-CORE (Class W)
capabilities.gate: pwsh scripts/verify-pins.ps1 && (cd impl/go && go test ./...)
capabilities.note: 255-vector standards-anchored corpus, non-circular (Wycheproof/FIPS-derived). §55 Class W. Core-wire ops now GRADED via non-circular RFC-anchored vectors (C4-C6): frame.seal/frame.open (whole-frame AEAD+AAD), nonce.derive, tlv.encode/tlv.decode, hkdf.expand/hkdf.expand_label — cross-validated by impl/go zz_*_xval_test.go against RFC 5116/5869 + RFC 8446 §7.1 (n-pamp prefix), closing the prior silent-skip gap in the Class-W pass.

capabilities.name: HANDSHAKE (Class H)
capabilities.gate: impl/_conformance-harness/kat-handshake-all-langs.sh
capabilities.note: Five standards-derived KATs, Standard profile. §55 Class H.

capabilities.name: RATCHET (Class H)
capabilities.gate: cd impl/go && GOWORK=off go test -run Ratchet ./...
capabilities.note: HTR master ratchet (#94): Tier-1 one-way HKDF-Expand-Label forward step + Tier-2 hybrid X25519MLKEM768 re-KEM self-heal on the SDK Conn root (masterSend/masterRecv + atomic gen counters), MASTER_RATCHET/REKEM Control-channel frames 0x0104-0x0107 + TLVRatchetGeneration 0x19. GRADED: non-circular Tier-1/Tier-2 KATs (independent stdlib-HKDF oracle re-anchored to RFC 5869 TC1 + RFC 8448, mutation-tested) + gen-0 byte-identical (5 handshake KATs unchanged) + real re-KEM over TCP loopback + race-clean; verified by maintainer GOWORK=off go test. Follow-on (tracked): spec/10 normative ratchet clauses + Nexus SDK-delegate wiring (design 93 step 9).

capabilities.name: NPAMP-MEMORY
capabilities.gate: cd impl/go && go test -run 'Memory|CBOR|Oracle' ./...
capabilities.note: GRADED: machine-gradable, non-circular conformance vectors exist -- test-vectors/gen/memory_oracle.py produces canonical CBOR independently of impl/go, and corpus ops memory.body.decode (8/8) + memory.body.encode (2/2) pass via npamp-conform (cross-validated by impl/go zz_oracle_xval_test.go). Reference impl is impl/go/memory*.go + memory_api.go; wired on this PR merge to main (NEP-0000 6a). First companion to reach graded.

capabilities.name: NPAMP-STREAM
capabilities.gate: cd impl/go && go test -run 'Stream|CBOR|Oracle' ./...
capabilities.note: GRADED (body-decode/envelope surface): the stream.body.decode op group (10/10) is graded by npamp-conform against an independent RFC 8949 oracle (test-vectors/gen/stream_oracle.py, canonical CBOR built independently of impl/go), cross-validated by impl/go/zz_stream_oracle_xval_test.go. Covers §4.1/4.2/4.3 payload+envelope MUST-reject incl. the STREAM sub_stream_id-is-uint divergence from MEMORY's corr-bytes. Reference impl impl/go/stream*.go. Behavioural §5-§9 clauses (flow control, state machine, reset) remain spec-audited (need a live-exchange harness). Second public companion to reach graded.

capabilities.name: NPAMP-SENSORY
capabilities.gate: cd impl/go && GOWORK=off go test -run Sensory ./...
capabilities.note: Native Sensory channel 0x0009 ops (min profile High). Spec-only in all 10 languages. Tracked gap.

capabilities.name: NPAMP-SPATIAL

capabilities.name: NPAMP-CAPABILITY
capabilities.gate: cd impl/go && GOWORK=off go test -run Capability ./...
capabilities.note: GRADED (payload body.decode surface): the capability.body.decode op group is graded by npamp-conform against an independent RFC 8949 oracle (test-vectors/gen/capability_oracle.py, non-circular), cross-validated by impl/go/zz_capability_oracle_xval_test.go (go test PASS). Reference impl impl/go/capability*.go. Behavioural clauses remain live-exchange-harness scope. Also graded in rust/typescript/python/php/ruby against the same corpus (6-language reference set incl. Go); each independently re-ran its toolchain (e.g. php test/BodyCorpusTest.php = 140/140).

capabilities.name: NPAMP-BRIDGE
capabilities.gate: cd impl/go && GOWORK=off go test -run Bridge ./...
capabilities.note: Protocol-agnostic Bridge framework, channel 0x000D. Spec §5.2: reserved corpus ops bridge.envelope.encode/decode/correlate carry ZERO vectors. Spec-only in all 10 languages. Tracked gap; oracle is the reserved-but-unpopulated corpus operations. GRADED (A2-BRIDGE): BridgeEnvelope encode/decode + correlation + structured error + MUST-reject (impl/go/bridge*.go) graded by non-circular RFC-8949 vectors (test-vectors/gen/bridge_oracle.py) -> corpus ops bridge.envelope.encode/decode + bridge.correlate, cross-validated by zz_bridge_oracle_xval_test.go (25 vectors, maintainer go test PASS). The carriage keystone: carriage classes + mappings build on this.
